things.name: Chatelherault Country Park
things.description: Stunning William Adam hunting lodge and country park above the dramatic River Avon gorge — home to the ancient white Cadzow cattle herd

things.name: Low Parks Museum
things.description: South Lanarkshire's principal museum covering Cameronians military history and local heritage from prehistoric times to the present

things.name: Hamilton Mausoleum
things.description: Extraordinary neoclassical mausoleum of the Dukes of Hamilton — famous for the world's longest echo, lasting up to 15 seconds

things.name: Strathclyde Country Park
things.description: Scotland's busiest country park with a 200-acre loch, watersports, Roman bathhouse remains and M&D's Scotland's Theme Park

things.name: Bothwell Castle
things.description: Scotland's finest 13th-century stone castle overlooking the Clyde — one of the great fortresses of medieval Europe

things.name: Hamilton Racecourse
things.description: Scotland's only flat horseracing track — a picturesque left-handed course in the Clyde valley with a full summer season of evening meetings

things.name: Calderglen Country Park & Zoo
things.description: Country park in the Calder Glen with a small zoo, children's farm, tropical house and extensive woodland walks

things.name: New Lanark World Heritage Site (Day Trip)
things.description: UNESCO mill village where social visionary Robert Owen built a model community — fully restored with a working mill and superb visitor centre

things.name: David Livingstone Centre (Blantyre)
things.description: Birthplace museum of explorer David Livingstone — the weaver's son who mapped the heart of Africa and found the Victoria Falls

things.name: Glasgow (Day Trip)
things.description: Scotland's largest city less than 30 minutes by train — world-class museums, food scene and the iconic Kelvingrove Art Gallery
